evals.query: I have a Kora service. Add a typed configuration for an 'app' section with name, version and environment fields, loaded from application.conf.
expected_behavior: Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:config-hocon dependency and the annotationProcessor ru.tinkoff.kora:annotation-processors
Declares @KoraApp interface extending HoconConfigModule
Creates an interface annotated @ConfigSource("app") with name(), version(), environment() methods
Creates src/main/resources/application.conf with a matching app { } section
Does not use field injection or value-placeholder annotations; reads config only through the constructor-injected @ConfigSource interface

evals.query: Externalize the database url, user and password through environment variables in my Kora HOCON config, with a sensible default pool size that can still be overridden.
expected_behavior: Uses required substitution ${DATABASE_URL} / ${DATABASE_USER} / ${DATABASE_PASSWORD}
Uses the default-then-override pattern (literal value reassigned with ${?VAR}) for pool size
Explains that ${VAR} is required and aborts startup if unset while ${?VAR} is optional
Does not rely on a ${VAR:default} placeholder as the substitution mechanism and does not read variables via System.getenv in code

evals.query: My Kora config interface needs a nested 'bar' object and a list of bars. How do I model the nested types?
expected_behavior: Keeps @ConfigSource only on the outer path-bound interface
Annotates the nested sub-object interfaces with @ConfigValueExtractor
Exposes the nested object as a method (e.g. BarConfig bar()) and the list as List<BarConfig> bars()
Does not annotate nested interfaces with @ConfigSource

evals.query: In my Kora @ConfigSource interface I need one required field, one optional field, and one field with a default value of 42.
expected_behavior: Uses a plain method for the required field (required by default in Kora)
Marks the optional field with @Nullable (e.g. jakarta.annotation.Nullable)
Provides the default via a Java default method returning 42
Does not use a @DefaultValue annotation (it does not exist in Kora)

evals.query: Two libraries in my Kora app share the same config shape (endpoint + requestTimeout) but live at libs.lib1 and libs.lib2. How do I bind both?
expected_behavior: Declares one shape with @ConfigValueExtractor (e.g. LibConfig)
Adds @Tag marker classes and @KoraApp default factory methods that call extractor.extract(config.get("libs.lib1")) and config.get("libs.lib2")
Injects ConfigValueExtractor<LibConfig> and ru.tinkoff.kora.config.common.Config into the factories
Optionally reuses one HOCON object (${common-lib}) overriding a single field
Distinguishes the two instances with @Tag (not field injection or any qualifier-by-name mechanism)

evals.query: How do I read a byte-size limit like 10Mb and a timeout like 250s into typed fields in a Kora config interface?
expected_behavior: Maps the byte size to ru.tinkoff.kora.config.common.Size
Maps the timeout to java.time.Duration with a value like "250s"
Notes that Kora has no DataSize type
Does not introduce a DataSize type or a custom string parser for the byte size

evals.query: I want a component in my Kora app to read only environment variables through the framework config abstraction.
expected_behavior: Injects ru.tinkoff.kora.config.common.Config with the @Environment tag
Mentions @SystemProperties and @ApplicationConfig as the other layer-scoping tags
Recommends typed @ConfigSource interfaces over injecting raw Config when possible
Does not use System.getenv directly as the primary approach instead of the @Environment-tagged Config

evals.query: How do I run my Kora service with a production config file instead of the default application.conf?
expected_behavior: Uses the config.resource system property for a classpath file or config.file for a filesystem path
Explains the load priority: config.resource, then config.file, then application.conf, then empty
Does not invent a config.environment or CONFIG_ENVIRONMENT profile switch (it does not exist in Kora)
